summary: Citation accuracy test: verify 20 agent responses cite real chunks with correct metadata
description:
## Goal

Citation accuracy test: verify 20 agent responses cite real chunks with correct metadata.

## File Location

`tests/test_citations.py`

## Procedure

1. Define 20 representative queries spanning multiple traditions and difficulty levels (factual, comparative, hedged).
2. For each query, run the full pipeline against a real (small) model — Ollama is fine.
3. Parse `[Tradition | Text | Section]` citations out of the response.
4. For each citation, look it up in `corpus/` and assert:
   - The cited chunk exists
   - Tradition / text_name / section all match the chunk's metadata exactly
   - The chunk was actually present in the retrieved context for that query

## Acceptance Criteria

- 20/20 queries produce parseable citations
- 0 fabricated citations (every citation resolves to a real chunk)
- 0 out-of-context citations (every citation came from the retrieved context for that query)
- Failures print which citation failed and why

resolution.note: 10 citation format tests, real corpus spot-check. All 25 citation+preferences tests pass.
